etymology.description: 一(一) (yī, "one") + 刻(刻) (kè, "quarter of an hour; moment; cut") = an instance of time as short as fifteen minutes -> a very brief period or "moment".
etymology.interpretation:
The sense of 'a moment' in 一刻 (yíkè) comes from 一(一) (yī, "one") suggesting a single unit, combined with 刻(刻) (kè, "quarter of an hour;
moment; cut"), which in classical usage can refer to a time division like 'quarter of an hour'. This yields the idea of a specific, very short span of time or 'a little while'. The association with 'moment' stems from the image of cutting/chiseling out a tiny portion of time.

parts.english: quarter of an hour; moment; cut

examples.chinese: 请等我一刻(請等我一刻)。
examples.english: Please wait for me a moment.

examples.chinese: 太阳出来一刻(太陽出來一刻)，大地暖和起来(大地暖和起來)。
examples.pinyin: Tàiyáng chūlái yíkè, dàdì nuǎnhé qǐlái.
examples.english: The moment the sun comes out, the earth warms up.

examples.chinese: 这一刻是值得珍惜的(這一刻是值得珍惜的)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè yíkè shì zhídé zhēnxī de.
